figures.caption: Table 14. SR-discovered formulas that outperform template formulas (F1–F42) on specific layer configurations.LOO R shown for relative degradation or log-odds targets.
Table 12. Summary of gplearn-discovered formulas. Train R and LOO R denote training and leave-one-outcorrelation coefficients.
Table 10. Summary of formula templates by category and variable count.
Table 1. Investigated variables. Performance metrics measure model quality, configuration parameters specifycompression settings, and derived quantities characterize weight matrix properties.
Table 16. Model comparison for attention layer scaling. Cubic polynomial (theoretically predicted) ranks #5.Root and logarithmic models rank near the bottom.
Table 17. Model comparison for MLP layer scaling. The √ γ model ranks #14, but power law γc ranks #7,suggesting sub-linear but not exactly square-root behavior.

Table 5. Best formulas by task across compression methods. WikiText evaluates perplexity prediction; allother tasks evaluate accuracy prediction. The Src column indicates whether the formula is from predefinedtemplates (F) or discovered via symbolic regression (D). Bold indicates best predictive performance (highestLOO R) per task.
Table 15. Best formulas discovered using transformed targets. Train R and LOO R (leave-one-out cross-validation) values shown. Formulas marked with † use entropy H (WikiText only).
Table 2. Predictor categories and template families for formula construction. k̄95 and k̄99 denote the meanrank required to capture 95% and 99% of spectral energy across weight matrices.
